::: {.chapter-num}
Chapter VI
:::

## Pacing & recovery {.chapter-title}

::: {.chapter-lede}
The body keeps a running tab.
What you do today often shows up two or three days later, the days that *do* knock you sideways have their own arc back to baseline, and a crash that drags on for a week is a different beast than one that lifts overnight.
Four views of the action-and-reaction loop that defines pacing — and because patterns shift over time, this chapter focuses on your **last 12 months** when your data spans more than 18 months.
The earlier chapters show the long view; this one tells you where you are *now*.
:::

::: {.analysis-section}

### The PEM window

Visible has four daily "demand" tags — *Physically active*, *Mentally demanding*, *Socially demanding*, *Emotionally stressful* — each rated 0–3.
This chart asks, for each demand type: when you do more of this, how does your stability score move *today*, *tomorrow*, *the day after*, and so on?
**Negative bars mean more demand → lower stability later.**
The highlighted bar is the lag where the relationship is strongest — your personal post-exertional window for that kind of strain.
If your worst dip lands consistently at +2 or +3 days, that's the gap you have to plan across.

```{webr}
#| label: fact-pem
#| input: ["fileText"]

if (exists("visible_recent")) {
  exertion_trackers <- c("Physically active", "Mentally demanding",
                         "Socially demanding", "Emotionally stressful")
  available_ex <- intersect(exertion_trackers, unique(visible_recent$tracker))

  if (length(available_ex) == 0) {
    htmltools::HTML(
      "<p class='section-fact'>No exertion-tag data found in your export.</p>"
    )
  } else {
    ex <- visible_recent |>
      filter(tracker %in% available_ex) |>
      group_by(odate, tracker) |>
      summarise(value = max(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
      pivot_wider(names_from = tracker, values_from = value)

    stab <- visible_recent |>
      filter(tracker == "Stability Score") |>
      group_by(odate) |>
      summarise(stab = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

    d_range <- range(visible_recent$odate, na.rm = TRUE)
    all_days <- data.frame(odate = seq.Date(d_range[1], d_range[2], "day"))
    joined <- all_days |>
      left_join(ex, by = "odate") |>
      left_join(stab, by = "odate") |>
      arrange(odate)

    rows <- list()
    for (tr in available_ex) {
      for (L in 0:4) {
        x <- joined[[tr]]
        y <- lead(joined$stab, L)
        ok <- !is.na(x) & !is.na(y)
        if (sum(ok) < 15) next
        r <- suppressWarnings(stats::cor(x[ok], y[ok]))
        if (is.na(r)) next
        rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
          tracker = tr, lag = L, r = r
        )
      }
    }

    if (length(rows) == 0) {
      htmltools::HTML(
        "<p class='section-fact'>Not enough overlapping days to compute lag.</p>"
      )
    } else {
      df <- do.call(rbind, rows)
      worst_overall <- df[which.min(df$r), ]

      htmltools::HTML(sprintf(
        paste0(
          '<p class="section-fact">',
          'Across <strong>%s</strong>, your strongest after-effect: <strong>%s</strong> ',
          'tends to drag stability down <strong>%d day%s later</strong> ',
          '(correlation %+0.2f, where -1 = perfectly opposite).',
          '</p>'
        ),
        recent_label,
        worst_overall$tracker,
        worst_overall$lag,
        if (worst_overall$lag == 1) "" else "s",
        worst_overall$r
      ))
    }
  }
}
```

```{webr}
#| label: chart-pem
#| input: ["fileText", "darkMode"]
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6

if (exists("visible_recent")) {
  viz <- viz_theme(darkMode)

  exertion_trackers <- c("Physically active", "Mentally demanding",
                         "Socially demanding", "Emotionally stressful")
  available_ex <- intersect(exertion_trackers, unique(visible_recent$tracker))

  if (length(available_ex) == 0) {
    cat("No exertion-tag trackers found — skipping.\n")
  } else {
    ex <- visible_recent |>
      filter(tracker %in% available_ex) |>
      group_by(odate, tracker) |>
      summarise(value = max(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
      pivot_wider(names_from = tracker, values_from = value)

    stab <- visible_recent |>
      filter(tracker == "Stability Score") |>
      group_by(odate) |>
      summarise(stab = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

    d_range <- range(visible_recent$odate, na.rm = TRUE)
    all_days <- data.frame(odate = seq.Date(d_range[1], d_range[2], "day"))
    joined <- all_days |>
      left_join(ex, by = "odate") |>
      left_join(stab, by = "odate") |>
      arrange(odate)

    rows <- list()
    for (tr in available_ex) {
      for (L in 0:4) {
        x <- joined[[tr]]
        y <- lead(joined$stab, L)
        ok <- !is.na(x) & !is.na(y)
        if (sum(ok) < 15) next
        r <- suppressWarnings(stats::cor(x[ok], y[ok]))
        if (is.na(r)) next
        rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
          tracker = tr, lag = L, r = r
        )
      }
    }

    if (length(rows) == 0) {
      cat("Not enough overlapping days to compute lag.\n")
    } else {
      df <- do.call(rbind, rows)
      df$best <- FALSE
      for (tr in unique(df$tracker)) {
        idx <- which(df$tracker == tr)
        df$best[idx[which.min(df$r[idx])]] <- TRUE
      }

      best_rows <- df[df$best, ]
      best_rows$label <- sprintf("%+d day%s",
                                  best_rows$lag,
                                  ifelse(best_rows$lag == 1, "", "s"))

      ggplot(df, aes(factor(lag), r, fill = best)) +
        geom_col(width = 0.75) +
        geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = viz$rule, linewidth = 0.4) +
        geom_text(data = best_rows,
                  aes(factor(lag), r, label = label),
                  vjust = ifelse(best_rows$r >= 0, -0.4, 1.3),
                  colour = viz$accent, size = 3,
                  fontface = "italic", show.legend = FALSE) +
        facet_wrap(~ tracker, ncol = 2) +
        scale_fill_manual(values = c(`TRUE` = viz$accent,
                                     `FALSE` = viz$ink_soft),
                          guide = "none") +
        labs(x = "Days after the busy day",
             y = "Relationship with stability",
             title = "When does today's exertion show up in your body?",
             subtitle = sprintf(
               "Based on %s · negative = more demand now, lower stability later · highlighted = strongest lag",
               recent_label)) +
        viz$theme(base_size = 11)
    }
  }
}
```

:::

::: {.analysis-section}

### Your personal ceiling

A simpler view of the same idea.
We bin every day by **how much you took on** — *quiet*, *light*, *moderate*, *busy*, *very busy* — based on the sum of your four demand tags.
Then we average **the next day's stability score** within each bin.
Reading top to bottom = doing more.
The vertical dashed line is your typical stability across all days; bars that sit clearly to the left of it are exertion levels where your body tends to push back the next day.
The number on each bar is how many days fell into that bin, so you can see which levels are well-evidenced and which are rare.

```{webr}
#| label: fact-envelope
#| input: ["fileText"]

if (exists("visible_recent")) {
  exertion_trackers <- c("Physically active", "Mentally demanding",
                         "Socially demanding", "Emotionally stressful")
  available_ex <- intersect(exertion_trackers, unique(visible_recent$tracker))

  if (length(available_ex) >= 2) {
    ex <- visible_recent |>
      filter(tracker %in% available_ex) |>
      group_by(odate, tracker) |>
      summarise(value = max(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
      pivot_wider(names_from = tracker, values_from = value)

    stab <- visible_recent |>
      filter(tracker == "Stability Score") |>
      group_by(odate) |>
      summarise(stab = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

    joined <- ex |>
      left_join(stab, by = "odate") |>
      arrange(odate)

    joined$total_ex <- rowSums(joined[, available_ex, drop = FALSE], na.rm = FALSE)
    joined$next_stab <- lead(joined$stab, 1)

    env <- joined[!is.na(joined$total_ex) & !is.na(joined$next_stab), ]

    if (nrow(env) >= 30) {
      med_stab <- stats::median(env$next_stab, na.rm = TRUE)
      env$ex_bin <- cut(env$total_ex,
                        breaks = c(-0.5, 0.5, 3.5, 6.5, 9.5, 12.5),
                        labels = c("Quiet (0)", "Light (1–3)",
                                   "Moderate (4–6)", "Busy (7–9)",
                                   "Very busy (10–12)"))
      bin_summary <- aggregate(next_stab ~ ex_bin, env, mean)
      bin_summary$n <- as.integer(table(env$ex_bin)[bin_summary$ex_bin])
      bin_summary <- bin_summary[bin_summary$n >= 3, ]

      if (nrow(bin_summary) < 2) {
        htmltools::HTML(sprintf(
          "<p class='section-fact'>Not enough variation in your daily exertion (%d day-pairs from %s) to compare bins.</p>",
          nrow(env), recent_label
        ))
      } else {
        gap <- max(bin_summary$next_stab) - min(bin_summary$next_stab)
        worst_bin <- bin_summary[which.min(bin_summary$next_stab), ]
        best_bin <- bin_summary[which.max(bin_summary$next_stab), ]

        htmltools::HTML(sprintf(
          paste0(
            '<p class="section-fact">',
            'Across <strong>%d</strong> day-pairs from %s, your tomorrow-stability runs ',
            '<strong>%.1f / 5</strong> after a <strong>%s</strong> day, ',
            'compared with <strong>%.1f / 5</strong> after a <strong>%s</strong> one — ',
            'a gap of <strong>%.1f points</strong>.',
            '</p>'
          ),
          nrow(env), recent_label,
          best_bin$next_stab, tolower(as.character(best_bin$ex_bin)),
          worst_bin$next_stab, tolower(as.character(worst_bin$ex_bin)),
          gap
        ))
      }
    }
  }
}
```

```{webr}
#| label: chart-envelope
#| input: ["fileText", "darkMode"]
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 5

if (exists("visible_recent")) {
  viz <- viz_theme(darkMode)

  exertion_trackers <- c("Physically active", "Mentally demanding",
                         "Socially demanding", "Emotionally stressful")
  available_ex <- intersect(exertion_trackers, unique(visible_recent$tracker))

  if (length(available_ex) < 2) {
    cat("Need at least two exertion tags to compute total daily demand.\n")
  } else {
    ex <- visible_recent |>
      filter(tracker %in% available_ex) |>
      group_by(odate, tracker) |>
      summarise(value = max(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
      pivot_wider(names_from = tracker, values_from = value)

    stab <- visible_recent |>
      filter(tracker == "Stability Score") |>
      group_by(odate) |>
      summarise(stab = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

    joined <- ex |>
      left_join(stab, by = "odate") |>
      arrange(odate)

    joined$total_ex <- rowSums(joined[, available_ex, drop = FALSE], na.rm = FALSE)
    joined$next_stab <- lead(joined$stab, 1)
    env <- joined[!is.na(joined$total_ex) & !is.na(joined$next_stab), ]

    if (nrow(env) < 30) {
      cat(sprintf("Only %d day-pairs available — need at least 30 to summarise.\n",
                  nrow(env)))
    } else {
      med_stab <- stats::median(env$next_stab, na.rm = TRUE)

      env$ex_bin <- cut(env$total_ex,
                        breaks = c(-0.5, 0.5, 3.5, 6.5, 9.5, 12.5),
                        labels = c("Quiet (0)", "Light (1–3)",
                                   "Moderate (4–6)", "Busy (7–9)",
                                   "Very busy (10–12)"))

      bin_summary <- env |>
        group_by(ex_bin) |>
        summarise(
          n = n(),
          mean_stab = mean(next_stab, na.rm = TRUE),
          se = stats::sd(next_stab, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
          .groups = "drop"
        ) |>
        filter(n >= 3)

      bin_summary$ex_bin <- factor(bin_summary$ex_bin,
                                    levels = rev(levels(bin_summary$ex_bin)))

      ggplot(bin_summary,
             aes(mean_stab, ex_bin)) +
        geom_vline(xintercept = med_stab, linetype = "dashed",
                   colour = viz$ink_soft, linewidth = 0.5) +
        annotate("text",
                 x = med_stab, y = Inf,
                 label = sprintf("typical day (%.1f)", med_stab),
                 vjust = -0.6, hjust = 0.5, size = 3,
                 colour = viz$muted, fontface = "italic") +
        geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = pmax(1, mean_stab - se),
                            xmax = pmin(5, mean_stab + se)),
                       height = 0.18, colour = viz$ink_soft,
                       linewidth = 0.5) +
        geom_point(colour = viz$accent, size = 4) +
        geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("n = %d", n)),
                  hjust = -0.3, nudge_x = 0.05,
                  colour = viz$muted, size = 3.2) +
        scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1, 5.6),
                           breaks = 1:5) +
        coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
        labs(x = "Tomorrow's stability score",
             y = NULL,
             title = "Stability the day after, by how busy today was",
             caption = sprintf(
               "Based on %s · dot is the average · whiskers show uncertainty · n = days in each bin",
               recent_label)) +
        viz$theme(base_size = 12) +
        theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
              plot.margin = margin(28, 25, 15, 15))
    }
  }
}
```

:::

::: {.analysis-section}

### Crash episodes

A crash that lasts a single day and a crash that drags on for a week are not the same event.
This chart treats every consecutive run of *Crash* days as one **episode**, and uses the run length as a rough severity score — the body's own measure of how hard it was.
Each bar is one episode; bar height is its length in days; colour groups episodes into mild (1 day), moderate (2–3 days) and severe (4+ days).
Clusters of bars close together suggest either a stretch where you were genuinely vulnerable, or a single underlying event the body kept re-flaring on.

```{webr}
#| label: fact-crash
#| input: ["fileText"]

if (exists("visible_recent") && "Crash" %in% visible_recent$tracker) {
  cr <- visible_recent[visible_recent$tracker == "Crash" &
                         !is.na(visible_recent$value), ]
  if (nrow(cr) == 0) {
    htmltools::HTML("<p class='section-fact'>No Crash entries logged.</p>")
  } else {
    daily <- aggregate(value ~ odate, cr, max)
    all_d <- data.frame(odate = seq.Date(min(cr$odate),
                                          max(cr$odate), 1))
    full <- merge(all_d, daily, by = "odate", all.x = TRUE)
    full <- full[order(full$odate), ]
    full$crashed <- !is.na(full$value) & full$value > 0

    if (!any(full$crashed)) {
      htmltools::HTML(sprintf(
        "<p class='section-fact'>No crash days logged in %s.</p>",
        recent_label
      ))
    } else {
      r <- rle(full$crashed)
      ends <- cumsum(r$lengths)
      starts <- c(1L, ends[-length(ends)] + 1L)
      ep_lengths <- r$lengths[r$values]
      ep_starts <- full$odate[starts[r$values]]
      worst_idx <- which.max(ep_lengths)

      mild <- sum(ep_lengths == 1)
      moderate <- sum(ep_lengths >= 2 & ep_lengths <= 3)
      severe <- sum(ep_lengths >= 4)

      htmltools::HTML(sprintf(
        paste0(
          '<p class="section-fact">',
          'In %s you had <strong>%d crash episode%s</strong> ',
          '(%d mild, %d moderate, %d severe). ',
          'Your worst lasted <strong>%d day%s</strong> starting <strong>%s</strong>.',
          '</p>'
        ),
        recent_label,
        length(ep_lengths),
        if (length(ep_lengths) == 1) "" else "s",
        mild, moderate, severe,
        ep_lengths[worst_idx],
        if (ep_lengths[worst_idx] == 1) "" else "s",
        format(ep_starts[worst_idx], "%a %d %b %Y")
      ))
    }
  }
} else if (exists("visible_recent")) {
  htmltools::HTML("<p class='section-fact'>No Crash tracker in your export — skip this section.</p>")
}
```

```{webr}
#| label: chart-crash
#| input: ["fileText", "darkMode"]
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 4.5

if (exists("visible_recent") && "Crash" %in% visible_recent$tracker) {
  viz <- viz_theme(darkMode)

  cr <- visible_recent[visible_recent$tracker == "Crash" &
                         !is.na(visible_recent$value), ]

  if (nrow(cr) == 0) {
    cat("No Crash entries logged.\n")
  } else {
    daily <- aggregate(value ~ odate, cr, max)
    all_d <- data.frame(odate = seq.Date(min(cr$odate),
                                          max(cr$odate), 1))
    full <- merge(all_d, daily, by = "odate", all.x = TRUE)
    full <- full[order(full$odate), ]
    full$crashed <- !is.na(full$value) & full$value > 0

    if (!any(full$crashed)) {
      cat(sprintf("No crash days in %s — nothing to draw.\n", recent_label))
    } else {
      r <- rle(full$crashed)
      ends <- cumsum(r$lengths)
      starts <- c(1L, ends[-length(ends)] + 1L)
      episodes <- data.frame(
        start = full$odate[starts[r$values]],
        length = r$lengths[r$values]
      )
      episodes$severity <- cut(episodes$length,
                                breaks = c(0, 1, 3, Inf),
                                labels = c("Mild (1 day)",
                                           "Moderate (2–3)",
                                           "Severe (4+)"))

      pal <- c(`Mild (1 day)` = viz$secondary,
               `Moderate (2–3)` = viz$highlight,
               `Severe (4+)` = viz$accent)

      worst_ep <- episodes[which.max(episodes$length), ]
      worst_ep$label <- sprintf("worst: %d days from %s",
                                 worst_ep$length,
                                 format(worst_ep$start, "%d %b"))

      ggplot(episodes, aes(start, length, fill = severity)) +
        geom_segment(aes(xend = start, yend = 0,
                         colour = severity),
                     linewidth = 0.6) +
        geom_point(aes(colour = severity), size = 2.4) +
        geom_text(data = worst_ep,
                  aes(start, length, label = label),
                  vjust = -1, hjust = 0.5,
                  colour = viz$accent, size = 3.2,
                  fontface = "italic",
                  inherit.aes = FALSE) +
        coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
        scale_fill_manual(values = pal, name = NULL, drop = FALSE) +
        scale_colour_manual(values = pal, name = NULL, drop = FALSE) +
        scale_y_continuous(breaks = function(lim) {
          seq(0, max(1, ceiling(lim[2])), by = 1)
        }) +
        labs(x = NULL, y = "Episode length (days)",
             title = "Crash episodes by length",
             subtitle = sprintf(
               "Each bar = one consecutive run of crash days · taller = more severe · %s",
               recent_label)) +
        viz$theme(base_size = 12) +
        theme(legend.position = "top",
              plot.margin = margin(15, 15, 10, 15))
    }
  }
}
```

:::

::: {.analysis-section}

### Recovery trajectory

For every "anomaly day" found in the previous chapter — the days when many of your trackers were off at once — this chart aligns the next two weeks side by side and averages them.
Day 0 is the bad day itself; the curves show the typical path back.
**All three lines are flipped so that "down" always means worse than usual** — that way Stability, HRV, and Resting HR can be read together.
If one curve climbs back to zero faster than the others, that measure is your *leading* recovery signal: it tells you the body is on the mend before the others catch up.

```{webr}
#| label: fact-recovery
#| input: ["fileText"]

if (exists("visible_mat_clean") && exists("visible") &&
    ncol(visible_mat_clean) >= 5) {
  mu <- colMeans(visible_mat_clean, na.rm = TRUE)
  sigma <- apply(visible_mat_clean, 2, stats::sd, na.rm = TRUE)
  sigma[sigma == 0] <- 1
  z <- sweep(sweep(visible_mat_clean, 2, mu, "-"), 2, sigma, "/")

  threshold <- max(3, ceiling(0.10 * ncol(visible_mat_clean)))
  spikes <- abs(z) > 2
  spikes[is.na(spikes)] <- FALSE
  spike_count <- rowSums(spikes)
  anomaly_dates <- as.Date(rownames(visible_mat_clean))[spike_count >= threshold]
  anomaly_dates <- anomaly_dates[anomaly_dates >= recent_cutoff]

  if (length(anomaly_dates) < 3) {
    htmltools::HTML(sprintf(
      "<p class='section-fact'>Only %d anomaly day(s) in %s — need at least 3 to draw recovery curves.</p>",
      length(anomaly_dates), recent_label
    ))
  } else {
    outcomes <- c("HR Variability", "Resting HR", "Stability Score")
    avail <- intersect(outcomes, unique(visible$tracker))

    od <- visible |>
      filter(tracker %in% avail) |>
      group_by(odate, tracker) |>
      summarise(value = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
      group_by(tracker) |>
      mutate(
        m = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
        s = stats::sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
        z = (value - m) / ifelse(s == 0, 1, s),
        z = ifelse(tracker == "Resting HR", -z, z)
      ) |>
      ungroup() |>
      select(odate, tracker, z)

    grid <- expand.grid(
      anomaly = anomaly_dates,
      days_after = 0:14,
      tracker = avail,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    grid$odate <- grid$anomaly + grid$days_after
    traj <- merge(grid, od, by = c("odate", "tracker"), all.x = TRUE)

    rec <- traj |>
      group_by(tracker, days_after) |>
      summarise(mean_z = mean(z, na.rm = TRUE),
                       n = sum(!is.na(z)),
                       .groups = "drop") |>
      filter(n >= 2)

    days_to_baseline <- function(df) {
      df <- df[order(df$days_after), ]
      day0 <- df$mean_z[df$days_after == 0]
      if (length(day0) == 0 || day0 >= -0.1) return(NA_integer_)
      back <- which(df$mean_z >= -0.1 & df$days_after > 0)
      if (length(back) == 0) return(NA_integer_)
      df$days_after[back[1]]
    }

    by_tracker <- split(rec, rec$tracker)
    recov <- vapply(by_tracker, days_to_baseline, integer(1))
    recov <- recov[!is.na(recov)]

    if (length(recov) == 0) {
      htmltools::HTML(sprintf(
        paste0(
          '<p class="section-fact">',
          'Across <strong>%d</strong> anomaly day(s) in %s, no measure cleanly returns to ',
          'its typical level within two weeks — recovery is slower than the window shown.',
          '</p>'
        ),
        length(anomaly_dates), recent_label
      ))
    } else {
      fastest <- names(recov)[which.min(recov)]
      slowest <- names(recov)[which.max(recov)]

      htmltools::HTML(sprintf(
        paste0(
          '<p class="section-fact">',
          'Across <strong>%d</strong> anomaly day(s) in %s, <strong>%s</strong> returns to ',
          'baseline fastest (~%d day%s), while <strong>%s</strong> takes longest (~%d day%s). ',
          'The faster-recovering measure is your earlier signal that the worst is over.',
          '</p>'
        ),
        length(anomaly_dates), recent_label,
        fastest, recov[fastest],
        if (recov[fastest] == 1) "" else "s",
        slowest, recov[slowest],
        if (recov[slowest] == 1) "" else "s"
      ))
    }
  }
}
```

```{webr}
#| label: chart-recovery
#| input: ["fileText", "darkMode"]
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 5

if (exists("visible_mat_clean") && exists("visible") &&
    ncol(visible_mat_clean) >= 5) {
  viz <- viz_theme(darkMode)

  mu <- colMeans(visible_mat_clean, na.rm = TRUE)
  sigma <- apply(visible_mat_clean, 2, stats::sd, na.rm = TRUE)
  sigma[sigma == 0] <- 1
  z <- sweep(sweep(visible_mat_clean, 2, mu, "-"), 2, sigma, "/")

  threshold <- max(3, ceiling(0.10 * ncol(visible_mat_clean)))
  spikes <- abs(z) > 2
  spikes[is.na(spikes)] <- FALSE
  spike_count <- rowSums(spikes)
  anomaly_dates <- as.Date(rownames(visible_mat_clean))[spike_count >= threshold]
  anomaly_dates <- anomaly_dates[anomaly_dates >= recent_cutoff]

  if (length(anomaly_dates) < 3) {
    cat(sprintf("Only %d anomaly day(s) in %s — need at least 3 to average a trajectory.\n",
                length(anomaly_dates), recent_label))
  } else {
    outcomes <- c("HR Variability", "Resting HR", "Stability Score")
    avail <- intersect(outcomes, unique(visible$tracker))

    od <- visible |>
      filter(tracker %in% avail) |>
      group_by(odate, tracker) |>
      summarise(value = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
      group_by(tracker) |>
      mutate(
        m = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
        s = stats::sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
        z = (value - m) / ifelse(s == 0, 1, s),
        z = ifelse(tracker == "Resting HR", -z, z)
      ) |>
      ungroup() |>
      select(odate, tracker, z)

    grid <- expand.grid(
      anomaly = anomaly_dates,
      days_after = 0:14,
      tracker = avail,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    grid$odate <- grid$anomaly + grid$days_after
    traj <- merge(grid, od, by = c("odate", "tracker"), all.x = TRUE)

    rec <- traj |>
      group_by(tracker, days_after) |>
      summarise(
        mean_z = mean(z, na.rm = TRUE),
        se = stats::sd(z, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(!is.na(z))),
        n = sum(!is.na(z)),
        .groups = "drop"
      ) |>
      filter(n >= 2)

    pal <- setNames(c(viz$accent, viz$secondary, viz$highlight)[seq_along(avail)],
                    avail)

    cross_zero <- function(df) {
      df <- df[order(df$days_after), ]
      day0 <- df$mean_z[df$days_after == 0]
      if (length(day0) == 0 || day0 >= 0) return(NULL)
      crossing <- which(df$mean_z >= 0 & df$days_after > 0)
      if (length(crossing) == 0) return(NULL)
      df[crossing[1], ]
    }
    crosses <- do.call(rbind, lapply(split(rec, rec$tracker), cross_zero))
    crosses_first <- if (!is.null(crosses) && nrow(crosses) > 0) {
      first_day <- min(crosses$days_after)
      crosses[crosses$days_after == first_day, ][1, ]
    } else NULL

    p <- ggplot(rec, aes(days_after, mean_z, colour = tracker, fill = tracker)) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = viz$rule, linewidth = 0.4) +
      geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = viz$ink_soft,
                 linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.4) +
      geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean_z - se, ymax = mean_z + se),
                  colour = NA, alpha = 0.18) +
      geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
      geom_point(size = 1.6) +
      scale_colour_manual(values = pal, name = NULL) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = pal, guide = "none") +
      scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 14, 2)) +
      labs(x = "Days after a hard day",
           y = "Distance from typical (lower = worse)",
           title = "How long does it take to bounce back?",
           subtitle = sprintf(
             "Averaged across %d anomaly day(s) in %s · all measures flipped so down = worse",
             length(anomaly_dates), recent_label)) +
      viz$theme(base_size = 12) +
      theme(legend.position = "top",
            plot.margin = margin(15, 25, 15, 15))

    if (!is.null(crosses_first)) {
      p <- p +
        geom_point(data = crosses_first,
                   aes(days_after, mean_z),
                   colour = pal[crosses_first$tracker],
                   fill = "white", shape = 21, size = 4, stroke = 1.4,
                   inherit.aes = FALSE) +
        geom_text(data = crosses_first,
                  aes(days_after, mean_z,
                      label = sprintf("%s back to baseline by day %d",
                                       tracker, days_after)),
                  hjust = -0.1, vjust = -0.8,
                  colour = pal[crosses_first$tracker],
                  size = 3.2, fontface = "italic",
                  inherit.aes = FALSE) +
        coord_cartesian(clip = "off")
    }
    p
  }
}
```

:::
